name: datree
description: Datree can be used on the command line to run policies against Kubernetes manifests YAML files or Helm charts

name: --help
-h
options.description: Show help for datree

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Internal configuration management for datree config file

name: -h
--help
options.description: Help for config

name: -h
--help
options.description: Get value for specific key from datree config.yaml file. Defaults to $HOME/.datree/config.yaml

name: -h
--help
options.description: Apply value for specific key in datree config.yaml file. Defaults to $HOME/.datree/config.yaml

name: -h
--help
options.description: Help for kustomize

name: --help
-h
options.description: Help for 'test'

options.name: --ignore-missing-schemas
options.description: Ignore missing schemas when executing schema validation step

options.name: --no-record
options.description: Do not send policy checks metadata to the backend

options.name: --only-k8s-files
options.description: Evaluate only valid yaml files with the properties 'apiVersion' and 'kind'. Ignore everything else

name: -o
--output
options.description: Define output format (simple, yaml, json, xml, JUnit)

name: -p
--policy
options.description: Policy name to run against

options.name: --policy-config
options.description: Path for local policies configuration file

options.name: --schema-location
options.description: Override schemas location search path (can be specified multiple times)

name: -s
--schema-version
options.description: Set kubernetes version to validate against. Defaults to 1.19.0

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Display 'How to Fix' link

subcommands.name: publish
subcommands.description: Publish policies configuration for given <fileName>. Input should be the path to the Policy-as-Code yaml configuration file. ## Note You need to first enable Policy-as-Code (PaC) on the settings page in the dashboard

name: -h
--help
options.description: Help for publish

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Trigger a policy check, provide a Kubernetes configuration file path or a glob pattern

-h
options.description: Help for 'test'
